functions.name: _compare_depth_to_camera
functions.signature: def _compare_depth_to_camera(image_path: str, bbox1: list, bbox2: list) -> int:
functions.docstring:
Compares the depth (cz) of two objects to determine which is closer to the camera.
Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    bbox1 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the first object.
    bbox2 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the second object.
Returns:
    int: 0 if bbox1 is closer, 1 if bbox2 is closer, -1 if equal distances.
functions.purpose: Returns 0 if the first object is closer to the camera, 1 if the second is closer, -1 if they are at the same distance.

functions.name: _compare_point_depth
functions.signature: def _compare_point_depth(image_path: str, point_a: list, point_b: list) -> int:
functions.docstring:
Compares the depth (distance from camera) of two points in the image to determine which is closer.
Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    point_a (list): The [x, y] coordinates of point A in pixel coordinates.
    point_b (list): The [x, y] coordinates of point B in pixel coordinates.
Returns:
    int: 0 if point_a is closer, 1 if point_b is closer, -1 if equal distances.
functions.purpose: Compares the metric depth of two specified pixel points and returns 0 if the first point is closer, 1 if the second is closer, or -1 if equal.

functions.name: _get_3d_distance
functions.signature: def _get_3d_distance(image_path: str, bbox1: list, bbox2: list) -> float:
functions.docstring:
Returns the Euclidean distance (in meters) between the 3D centers of two objects identified by their 2D bounding boxes.
Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    bbox1 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the first object.
    bbox2 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the second object.
Returns:
    float: The Euclidean distance in meters.
functions.purpose: Computes the absolute 3D Euclidean distance between two objects specified by their 2D bounding boxes.
functions.code_body:
def _get_3d_distance(image_path: str, bbox1: list, bbox2: list) -> float:

    import math

    pos1 = get_3d_position(image_path, bbox1)
    pos2 = get_3d_position(image_path, bbox2)

    if pos1 is None or pos2 is None:
        return 0.0

    cx1, cy1, cz1 = pos1[0]
    cx2, cy2, cz2 = pos2[0]

    dx = cx2 - cx1
    dy = cy2 - cy1
    dz = cz2 - cz1

    distance = math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)
    return distance

functions.name: _get_horizontal_distance
functions.signature: def _get_horizontal_distance(image_path: str, bbox1: list, bbox2: list) -> float:
functions.docstring:
Computes the absolute horizontal distance (difference in cx coordinates) between two objects.
Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    bbox1 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the first object.
    bbox2 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the second object.
Returns:
    float: The horizontal distance in meters (absolute difference).
functions.purpose: Computes the absolute horizontal distance between two objects given their 2D bounding boxes.

functions.name: _get_shortest_distance_between_objects
functions.signature: def _get_shortest_distance_between_objects(image_path: str, boundingbox1: list, boundingbox2: list) -> float:
functions.docstring:
Computes the shortest surface distance between two 3D objects using their 2D bounding boxes (based on AABB assumption).
Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    boundingbox1 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the first object.
    boundingbox2 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the second object.
Returns:
    float: The shortest distance in meters. Returns 0.0 if objects intersect.
functions.purpose: Computes the shortest 3D surface distance between two objects given their 2D bounding boxes.
functions.code_body:
def _get_shortest_distance_between_objects(image_path: str, boundingbox1: list, boundingbox2: list) -> float:

    import math

    pos1_res = get_3d_position(image_path, boundingbox1)
    dim1_res = get_3d_dimensions(image_path, boundingbox1)

    pos2_res = get_3d_position(image_path, boundingbox2)
    dim2_res = get_3d_dimensions(image_path, boundingbox2)

    cx1, cy1, cz1 = pos1_res[0]
    dx1, dy1, dz1 = dim1_res[0]
    
    cx2, cy2, cz2 = pos2_res[0]
    dx2, dy2, dz2 = dim2_res[0]
    
    dist_x = max(0.0, abs(cx1 - cx2) - (dx1 + dx2) / 2.0)
    dist_y = max(0.0, abs(cy1 - cy2) - (dy1 + dy2) / 2.0)
    dist_z = max(0.0, abs(cz1 - cz2) - (dz1 + dz2) / 2.0)

    shortest_distance = math.sqrt(dist_x**2 + dist_y**2 + dist_z**2)
    
    return shortest_distance

functions.name: _get_object_height
functions.docstring:
Returns the height (dz) of an object specified by a 2D bounding box.
Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    boundingbox (list): A 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] in pixel coordinates.
Returns:
    float: The height of the object in meters.
functions.purpose: Returns the height (vertical dimension) of an object given its 2D bounding box.

functions.name: _get_object_width
functions.docstring:
Returns the width (dy) of an object specified by a 2D bounding box, extracted from get_3d_dimensions.

Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    boundingbox (list): A 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] in pixel coordinates.

Returns:
    float: The width (dy) of the object in meters.
functions.purpose: Returns the width (dy) of an object given its 2D bounding box, extracted from get_3d_dimensions.

functions.name: _get_point_depth_meters
functions.docstring:
Returns the metric depth (distance from camera in meters) of a specific point in the image.

Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    point (list): The [x, y] coordinates of the target point in absolute pixel coordinates.

Returns:
    float: The metric depth of the point in meters from the camera.
functions.purpose: Returns the metric depth in meters of a specified pixel point in the image.

functions.name: _get_vertical_distance
functions.signature: def _get_vertical_distance(image_path: str, bbox1: list, bbox2: list) -> float:
functions.docstring:
Computes the absolute vertical distance (difference in cy from get_3d_position) between two objects.

This function uses the get_3d_position primitive to retrieve the 3D center coordinates of each object, then extracts the cy value (index 1) from the returned list to compute the absolute difference.

Args:
    image_path (str): Absolute path to the image file on disk.
    bbox1 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the first object.
    bbox2 (list): 2D bounding box [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] of the second object.

Returns:
    float: The absolute vertical distance in meters (|cy1 - cy2|).
functions.purpose: Computes the absolute vertical distance between two objects using their 3D center coordinates (cy values) obtained from their 2D bounding boxes.
